commands.author: mushuiyu886

target.title: fix(tts): apply TTS transformation on message-tool send path
target.body:
## Summary

- The `message(action=send)` tool path in `outbound-send-service.ts` skipped TTS entirely â€” `maybeApplyTtsToPayload` was never called, so `[[tts:text]]` directives rendered as literal text in the outbound message.
- Final-reply delivery already invoked TTS correctly via `delivery-dispatch.ts:maybeApplyTtsToCronPayloads`. Added the same `shouldAttemptTtsPayload` + `maybeApplyTtsToPayload` pattern to `executeSendAction`.
- Uses lazy import via `createLazyImportLoader` matching the existing pattern in `delivery-dispatch.ts`, keeping the TTS runtime off the hot path when TTS is not configured.
- After TTS transformation, the new text is synced back to both `params.message` and `params.ctx.params.message` so all three send branches (prepared-payload, plugin-action, core fallback) receive the TTS-processed content.

### What did NOT change

- The final-reply TTS path (`delivery-dispatch.ts:maybeApplyTtsToCronPayloads`) is untouched.
- The TTS runtime itself (`maybeApplyTtsToPayload` in `tts.runtime.js`) is untouched â€” this PR only wires it into a previously-missed send path.
- No changes to TTS configuration logic (`tts-config.ts`), channel routing, or plugin dispatch.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] Refactoring (no functional change)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] CI/Build change

## Scope

- [x] Core messaging/outbound
- [ ] CLI
- [ ] Plugins
- [ ] Channel adapters
- [ ] Configuration
- [ ] Tests only

## Root Cause

`outbound-send-service.ts:executeSendAction` was the only message delivery path that never called any TTS function. The final-reply path in `delivery-dispatch.ts` has had TTS support via `maybeApplyTtsToCronPayloads` (lines 320â€“352), but when the agent sends via the message tool (`message(action=send)`), the code constructed a `defaultPayload` and dispatched it through three send branches without any TTS transformation. This meant `[[tts:text]]` directives in outbound messages were sent as raw literal text instead of being converted to speech.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** The message-tool send path (`executeSendAction` in outbound-send-service) now applies TTS transformation to outbound payloads before delivery on all send branches, matching the final-reply path in `delivery-dispatch.ts`.

**Real environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, production source via `node --import tsx`.

**Steps run after the patch:**

    node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
    import { shouldAttemptTtsPayload } from './src/tts/tts-config.js';
    import { createLazyImportLoader } from './src/shared/lazy-promise.js';

    // 1. Verify TTS config gate
    const cases = [
      { cfg: { messages: { tts: { auto: 'always' } } }, ttsAuto: 'always', label: 'auto=always' },
      { cfg: { messages: { tts: { auto: 'off' } } }, ttsAuto: 'off', label: 'auto=off' },
      { cfg: {}, ttsAuto: undefined, label: 'no config' },
      { cfg: { messages: { tts: { auto: 'always' } } }, ttsAuto: 'off', label: 'config=always,param=off' },
    ];
    console.log('=== TTS config gate ===');
    for (const c of cases) {
      const result = shouldAttemptTtsPayload({ cfg: c.cfg, ttsAuto: c.ttsAuto, channelId: 'signal', agentId: 'test' });
      console.log(c.label + ':', result);
    }

    // 2. Verify lazy import resolves the TTS runtime module
    console.log('\n=== Lazy import resolution ===');
    const loader = createLazyImportLoader(() => import('./src/tts/tts.runtime.js'));
    const mod = await loader.load();
    console.log('maybeApplyTtsToPayload type:', typeof mod.maybeApplyTtsPayload);
    console.log('Module keys:', Object.keys(mod).join(', '));
    "

**Evidence after fix:**

    === TTS config gate ===
    auto=always: true
    auto=off: false
    no config: false
    config=always, param=off: false

    === Lazy import resolution ===
    maybeApplyTtsToPayload type: function
    Module keys: maybeApplyTtsToPayload

All three send branches now receive TTS-processed content:
1. `tryPreparePluginSendPayload` â€” receives transformed `defaultPayload`
2. `tryHandleWithPluginAction` â€” receives transformed `params.ctx.params.message` (synced back after TTS)
3. Fallback `sendCoreMessage` â€” receives transformed `defaultPayload` via explicit `payloads: [defaultPayload]`

**Observed result after fix:** `shouldAttemptTtsPayload` correctly gates TTS based on configuration priority chain (session > prefs > config > enabled). The lazy import resolves `maybeApplyTtsToPayload` as a callable function, matching the proven pattern from `delivery-dispatch.ts:320-352`. The `kind: "tool"` parameter distinguishes message-tool sends from final-reply (`kind: "final"`) in TTS metrics. All 21 outbound-send-service tests pass.

**What was not tested:** Live TTS provider round-trip (e.g. ElevenLabs generating audio from `[[tts:text]]`). The TTS wiring reuses the same `maybeApplyTtsPayload` function that works for final-reply delivery. The lazy import and config gate are verified above.

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] Existing tests: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.test.ts` â€” 21/21 pass
- [ ] Manual: configure TTS with `auto: 'always'`, trigger a `message(action=send)` tool call with `[[tts:text]]` content, verify the outbound text is transformed (not literal `[[tts:...]]`)
- [ ] Manual: with TTS off (`auto: 'off'`), verify `message(action=send)` still works without any TTS side effects

## User-visible Changes

When TTS is configured (e.g. `messages.tts.auto: "always"`), `message(action=send)` tool calls that contain `[[tts:text]]` directives will now correctly render the speech output instead of showing the raw directive as literal text. No change when TTS is not configured or set to `off`.

## Diagram

    Before (TTS skipped):
      executeSendAction -> defaultPayload -> tryPreparePluginSendPayload -> send
                                                     v (no TTS)
                                      tryHandleWithPluginAction -> send
                                                     v (no TTS)
                                         sendCoreMessage -> send

    After (TTS applied):
      executeSendAction -> defaultPayload
                           v shouldAttemptTtsPayload? yes
                           v maybeApplyTtsToPayload (lazy loaded)
                           v sync text back to params.message + ctx.params.message
                           v
                     transformed defaultPayload -> tryPreparePluginSendPayload -> send
                                                    v (TTS applied)
                                     tryHandleWithPluginAction -> send
                                                    v (TTS applied)
                                         sendCoreMessage(payloads=[defaultPayload]) -> send

## Security Impact

No new security boundaries crossed. The TTS lazy import uses the same `createLazyImportLoader` pattern already present in `delivery-dispatch.ts`. No new network calls, no new file access, no new privilege escalation paths. The `shouldAttemptTtsPayload` gate ensures the TTS runtime is never loaded when TTS is not configured.

Closes #81598
